content:
**May 19, 2025 – Bucharest**

**Reformist Candidate Andrei Popescu Wins Romanian Presidency After Historic Runoff**

Andrei Popescu, the reformist candidate from the Save Romania Union (USR), has emerged victorious in Romania’s unprecedented presidential election re-run, defeating Social Democratic Party (PSD) candidate Gabriela Ionescu in the May 18 runoff. The election follows months of political uncertainty after the annulment of the November 2024 first round by Romania’s Constitutional Court, citing irregularities.

Popescu secured 54.2% of the vote, according to the Central Electoral Bureau’s preliminary count, against Ionescu’s 45.8%. Voter turnout reached 59%, reflecting a renewed public interest after the tumultuous restart of the electoral process.

“This is a victory for democracy and the rule of law,” Popescu declared to jubilant supporters in Bucharest’s University Square. “Romanians have shown they want change and a government that listens.”

Popescu, 44, campaigned on pledges to strengthen anti-corruption institutions, accelerate EU integration, and overhaul the healthcare and education systems. Ionescu congratulated her opponent and urged “unity and respect for democratic values in the years ahead.”

Prime Minister Alina Preda, who led the coalition government that oversaw the election re-run, praised the peaceful conduct of the vote. “Romania has demonstrated resilience and a commitment to democratic norms,” she said.

Popescu will be sworn in next month, marking a generational shift and signaling possible reforms amid persistent economic and political challenges.
